codeReview.summary: Implementation covers the feature requirements: SQLCipher-backed persistence is in place, wrong/missing key scenarios fail closed via explicit error types, no plaintext fallback path is introduced, and key artifacts are written/validated with owner-only permissions. Test coverage in the commit includes focused security cases for VAL-SEC-001/003/004/005.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mors-worker skill does not document the package-specific caveat that SQLCipher keying is not supported on in-memory DBs for better-sqlite3-multiple-ciphers, which caused implementation churn during preflight work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly calls this out (handoff file line 197). The resulting implementation comment in src/store.ts notes the same caveat (commit 6dd425b, src/store.ts lines 42-43).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library environment docs capture SQLCipher as a dependency but not the in-memory preflight limitation, so future workers may rediscover this behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/environment.md documents SQLCipher requirement generically, while commit 6dd425b uses a temp-file preflight path specifically because in-memory keying is unsupported (src/store.ts lines 42-49).

summary: Reviewed feature core-sqlcipher-store-and-security-invariants at commit 6dd425b; code and tests satisfy scoped security invariants with no blocking defects found. Noted a skill/library documentation gap around SQLCipher preflight behavior for better-sqlite3-multiple-ciphers.
